summary: Antral follicle count (AFC) effectively assesses ovarian reserve and correlates well with response to ovulation induction, particularly indicating high responsiveness when above 20 or poor reserves under five. Yet, existing literature does not provide conclusive proof that AFC outperforms age or FSH in predicting real-world IVF success such as live birth rates; thus its superiority remains unverified despite offering clinically meaningful data.
